target.name: Twelve
target.founder: Nicholas Flanders, Etosha Cave, Kendra Kuhl

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Twelve is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised over $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector comparables: KoBold Metals ($3.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M), supplemented by a broader universe of eight private climate-tech companies and one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $1.0B raised proxy, implies a valuation of $1.7B. The peer-median method across the three direct comparables yields $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B, reflecting material variance in how the market values Series C climate technologies. These methods converge on a range of $850M to $2.0B, with a median implied valuation of $1.4B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850M–$2.0B, centered at $1.4B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations—a 4.3x spread from minimum to maximum—and the absence of recent public market comparables, limiting granularity on how public markets currently price mature climate-tech risk. The stage-progression method's reliance on a standardized multiplier may not capture Twelve's specific technology profile or capital efficiency relative to peers.
